title: HelloMeds Backup
description: Schema for HelloMeds medication data backup files. The JSON payload is either stored as plain text (.hmbackup) or encrypted with AES-256-GCM using a user-provided passphrase. See docs/backups.md for the binary encryption format.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference. Included for tooling support (editor autocompletion, validation).

version.description: Backup format version. Importers should warn on unknown versions but still attempt import. Current: 1.

appVersion.description: The HelloMeds app version that created this backup (e.g., '1.0.0'). Informational only.

exportedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when this backup was created. Used for display and conflict resolution.

importanceLabels.description: Importance labels define notification behavior for medications. Each medication references a label by name. Labels must be imported before medications that reference them.

medications.description: The medication entries in this backup. Each medication contains nested schedules, history, and stock data.

importanceLabel.description: An importance label controls how aggressively the app reminds the user about a medication. Labels range from 'silent' (no reminders) to 'critical with follow-ups' (escalating alerts).

name.description: Display name of the label. Must be unique within the backup. Medications reference this name, not an ID.

shouldRemind.description: Whether medications with this label trigger notifications at all. When false, no reminders are sent.

isCritical.description: Whether notifications use the critical/high-priority channel. On Android this can bypass Do Not Disturb. On iOS this uses time-sensitive interruption level.

hasFollowUps.description: Whether the app sends follow-up reminders if the user hasn't responded to the initial notification.

followUpCount.description: Number of follow-up reminders to send. Only meaningful when hasFollowUps is true.

followUpIntervalMinutes.description: Minutes between each follow-up reminder. Only meaningful when hasFollowUps is true.

criticalAfterFollowUp.description: If set, the notification channel escalates to critical after this many follow-ups have been sent. Null means no escalation.

medication.description: A single medication with its visual appearance, schedules, intake history, and stock tracking data.

name.description: The medication name as it appears on the packaging (e.g., 'Ibuprofen 400mg'). Required and must not be blank.

displayName.description: An optional user-defined display name shown instead of 'name' in the UI (e.g., 'Morning pain pill'). If null, the name field is displayed.

type.description: The physical form of the medication. Determines the default icon shape. Unknown values should fall back to TABLET.

shape.description: Legacy shape identifier. Superseded by foregroundShape/backgroundShape but retained for backward compatibility.

notes.description: Free-text user notes about this medication. May contain personal information.

isArchived.description: Whether the user has archived this medication. Archived medications are hidden from the main tracking screen but retained for history.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when this medication was first added to the app.

foregroundShape.description: The foreground icon shape rendered on top of the background shape. Represents the medication's physical appearance.

backgroundShape.description: The background container shape behind the foreground icon. Purely decorative.

shapeColor.description: Primary color for the medication icon, as a MedicationColor enum name (e.g., 'RED', 'BLUE'). Null uses the theme default.

strengthValue.description: Numeric strength of the medication (e.g., 400 for '400mg'). Null if strength is not tracked.

strengthUnit.description: Unit for the strength value. Combined with strengthValue for display (e.g., '400 MG'). Null if not tracked.

importanceLabel.description: The name of the importance label assigned to this medication. Must match an entry in the top-level importanceLabels array. Controls notification behavior.

displayOrder.description: Sort order in the medication list. Lower values appear first. Users can reorder medications via drag-and-drop.

timeZoneMode.description: LOCAL (default): schedule times adjust to the user's current timezone when traveling. FIXED: schedule times are anchored to the origin timezone — recommended for birth control or antibiotics where exact intervals matter.

cycleType.description: NONE (default): standard medication with no cycle. CYCLIC: medication follows an active/break day pattern (e.g., birth control blister packs). When CYCLIC, the schedule projector masks events on break days.

cycleDaysActive.description: Number of active days per cycle (e.g., 21 for a 21/7 cycle). Must be > 0 when cycleType is CYCLIC. Null when not cyclic.

cycleDaysBreak.description: Number of break/off days per cycle (e.g., 7 for a 21/7 cycle). Must be >= 0 when cycleType is CYCLIC. Null when not cyclic.

cycleHasPlacebos.description: Whether break days include placebo pills. When true, reminders still appear on break days but are marked as placebo. When false, break day events are suppressed entirely.

cycleStartDate.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) anchoring the cycle. Day 0 of each cycle aligns to this date. The cycle advances by one calendar day regardless of missed doses or schedule frequency.

stock.description: Stock tracking configuration. Null if the user has not enabled stock tracking for this medication.

schedules.description: Dosing schedules. Each schedule defines a recurring time and dose. A medication can have multiple schedules (e.g., morning and evening).

history.description: Medication intake history (taken/skipped/auto-skipped events). Optional — manual exports may exclude this to reduce file size.

stockAdjustments.description: Stock adjustment ledger. Tracks refills, intakes, and manual corrections. Optional — included when history is included.

stockSettings.description: Configuration for tracking how much medication the user has remaining. Supports exact dose counting or estimated container tracking.

trackingEnabled.description: Whether stock tracking is active. When false, the remaining fields are ignored.

trackingPrecision.description: EXACT counts individual doses. ESTIMATED tracks containers (e.g., 'bottle is half full'). Null if not configured.

currentStockQuantity.description: Current stock level. For EXACT: number of doses remaining. For ESTIMATED: number of containers. Updated automatically as doses are logged.

lowStockThreshold.description: When currentStockQuantity drops below this value, the app shows a low-stock warning.

packagingQuantity.description: Number of doses per package/container. Used to calculate run-out predictions.

medicationContainer.description: The type of container this medication comes in. Affects the stock tracking icon and terminology.

depletionReminderEnabled.description: Whether to send a notification when stock runs low.

containerStartedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the current container was opened. Used for estimated depletion calculations.

schedule.description: A recurring dosing schedule. Defines when and how much medication to take. The app generates projected events from these schedules — events are never stored in the database.

id.description: Local schedule ID from the source database. Used during import to map history entries to their schedules. The importing app assigns new IDs and maintains the mapping.

dose.description: Number of doses to take at each scheduled time (e.g., 2 for 'take 2 tablets').

startDate.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) when this schedule becomes active. Events are not generated before this date.

endDate.description: ISO-8601 date when this schedule expires. Null means the schedule runs indefinitely. Past end dates effectively archive the schedule.

timeOfDay.description: Time of day in HH:mm format (24-hour clock). The notification fires at this local time.

frequencyType.description: INTERVAL: repeat every N days (e.g., daily, every 2 days). DAYS_OF_WEEK: repeat on specific weekdays.

frequencyValue.description: For INTERVAL: the number of days between doses (1 = daily, 2 = every other day). Ignored for DAYS_OF_WEEK.

daysOfWeek.description: Comma-separated day names (e.g., 'MON,WED,FRI') when frequencyType is DAYS_OF_WEEK. Null for INTERVAL schedules.

isArchived.description: Whether the user has manually archived this schedule. Archived schedules stop generating events but are retained for history.

originTimeZone.description: IANA timezone ID (e.g., 'America/New_York') where this schedule was created. Used when the medication's timeZoneMode is FIXED to anchor reminder times. Null for LOCAL mode schedules.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when this schedule was created.

historyEntry.description: A single medication intake event. Records whether a dose was taken, skipped, or auto-skipped (missed and automatically marked).

scheduleId.description: The schedule ID this history entry belongs to. Maps to a schedule's 'id' field within the same medication. Null for as-needed doses that don't follow a schedule.

scheduledTime.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the dose was scheduled. Null for as-needed doses.

takenTime.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the user actually took the dose. Null if the dose was skipped.

scheduledDose.description: The dose that was scheduled (from the schedule's dose field).

actualDose.description: The dose the user actually took, if different from the scheduled dose. Null means the scheduled dose was taken as-is.

status.description: TAKEN: user confirmed they took the dose. SKIPPED: user explicitly skipped. AUTO_SKIPPED: the app automatically marked as missed after the catch-up window expired.

notes.description: User notes about this specific dose (e.g., 'took with food'). May contain personal information.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when this record was created in the database.

stockAdjustment.description: A single stock level change. The stock system works as a ledger: the current stock is the sum of all adjustments.

quantityChange.description: The change in stock quantity. Positive for refills, negative for intakes. For example, -1 means one dose was taken; +30 means a refill of 30 doses.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when this adjustment occurred.

adjustmentType.description: INTAKE: dose taken (auto-generated when logging). REFILL: user added stock. MANUAL_CORRECTION: user corrected the count. INITIAL_STOCK: first stock entry. CONTAINER_DEPLETED: user marked a container as empty.

notes.description: User notes about this adjustment (e.g., 'picked up prescription'). May contain personal information.
